cleaning_log.description: Filled 22 missing values in 'margin_pct' with median

summary: Revenue increased 12.4% over the analyzed period, indicating improving top-line performance.
Margin percentage declined 4.1% in Q3 despite revenue growth, suggesting cost pressure or discounting.
Revenue volatility rose in late Q3; investigate campaign timing or supply constraints.

wow_findings.title: Margin declined despite revenue growth
wow_findings.evidence: Revenue +12.4% while margin_pct trend -4.1% in Q3

executive_insights.text: Top-line growth is positive: revenue rose 12.4% over the period.
Profitability risk: margin fell ~4.1% in Q3 while revenue grew, indicating cost pressure or discounting.
North region contributes the highest volume (420 records) and appears to be the primary growth driver.
Revenue has 18 high-end outliers; a small number of deals may be disproportionately impacting averages.
Late Q3 shows higher volatility; align supply and marketing cadence to stabilize performance.
executive_insights.evidence: trend summary: volatility rose in late Q3

risks.text: Margin compression could continue if cost inflation persists.
Outlier-driven performance may mask underlying segment weakness.
Regional dependency on North may increase exposure to local disruptions.

opportunities.text: Run a pricing/discount audit for Q3 campaigns to restore margin.
Replicate NorthΓÇÖs best-performing playbooks in underperforming regions.
Create a 'large-deal' pipeline view to monitor outliers separately.

actions.text: Review cost drivers and discounting in Q3; set guardrails on promotions.
Segment performance by region and product; prioritize fixes for lowest-margin segments.
Add a large-deal flag and report separately to avoid average distortion.

pipeline.note: Dropped 1 empty column, filled 22 margin_pct values, removed 7 duplicates
Executive insights, risks, opportunities, and actions generated

issues: 18 high-end revenue outliers may skew averages
strengths: No missing values in KPI columns
Consistent revenue growth over the analyzed period

warnings.message: Revenue distribution contains 18 high-end outliers that may distort averages.
Margin percentage declined in Q3 while revenue increased.

chat_context.dataset_brief: Daily revenue, cost, units, and margin percentage by region and product for a single quarter of sales.
evidence_pack: Revenue increased 12.4% over the analyzed period.
Margin percentage declined around 4.1 points in Q3.
North region has the highest number of records (420) and leads revenue.
18 revenue outliers detected via IQR with an upper bound around 195,000.
suggested_questions: Which regions contribute most to revenue and margin?
How do margin trends differ by region and product?
What is the impact of outlier deals on overall performance?
Where should we focus to restore margin without hurting growth?
